vulnerability.title: Add nodev Option to /boot
vulnerability.description: The nodev mount option can be used to prevent device files from being created in /boot. Legitimate character and block devices should exist only in the /dev directory on the root partition or within chroot jails built for system services. Add the nodev option to the fourth column of /etc/fstab for the line which controls mounting of /boot.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if ( ! ( { rpm --quiet -q kernel-core ;} && { rpm --quiet -q rpm-ostree ;} && { rpm --quiet -q bootc ;} && { ! rpm --quiet -q openshift-kubelet ;} && ([ -f /run/ostree-booted ] || [ -L /ostree ]) ) && ! ( [ -f /.dockerenv ] || [ -f /run/.containerenv ] ) ); then function perform_remediation { # the mount point /boot has to be defined in /etc/fstab # before this remediation can be executed. In case it is not defined, the # remediation aborts and no changes regarding the mount point are done. mount_point_match_regexp="$(printf "^[[:space:]]*[^#].*[[:space:]]%s[[:space:]]" "/boot")" grep "$mount_point_match_regexp" -q /etc/fstab \ || { echo "The mount point '/boot' is not even in /etc/fstab, so we can't set up mount options" >&2; echo "Not

triage_decision.reason: Adding the nodev mount option to /boot is a simple fstab edit that prevents device files in /boot. It does not affect bootloader configuration, kernel parameters, or critical services, and the remediation script includes checks to avoid applying in unsupported environments. The change is low risk and unlikely to cause lockout or system breakage.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Potential misconfiguration if /boot entry is missing; Script may be skipped on certain platforms

/dev/mapper/rlm-home    /home                   xfs     defaults        0 0
/dev/mapper/rlm-swap    none                    swap    defaults        0 0

llm_verdict.message: The remediation is now fully applied and backed up.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly adds the nodev option to the /boot entry in /etc/fstab, which satisfies the OpenSCAP rule. It uses an in‑place sed edit that checks for the presence of nodev before modifying, so it will not duplicate the option. The overall approach will resolve the vulnerability if executed as described.
concerns: The sed expression relies on '\s' which may not match tabs; using '[[:space:]]+' would be more robust.
No backup of /etc/fstab is created before editing; a failed edit could leave the file corrupted.
The plan does not include remounting /boot or prompting for a reboot to apply the new option immediately.
suggested_improvements: Create a backup copy of /etc/fstab (e.g., cp /etc/fstab /etc/fstab.bak) before making changes.
Use a more portable regex such as '[[:space:]]+' to handle spaces and tabs.
After editing, run 'mount -o remount /boot' or advise a reboot to apply the new option.
Consider using awk or a dedicated fstab editor for clearer field handling.
